title: QUIC Client Certificate Acceptance Policy Verification
verification_method: Focused requirement-home tests and inspection evidence for the client-side certificate-acceptance seam, its pinned-fingerprint policy, and the tightened peer transport-parameter commit gate.
expected_result: The bridge accepts a pinned leaf SHA-256 fingerprint only after the peer CertificateVerify proof exists, leaves no-policy configurations blocked without silent acceptance, rejects mismatched pinned fingerprints through the existing fatal path, surfaces PeerCertificatePolicyAccepted explicitly when the pin matches, and does not allow peer transport-parameter commit until proof, policy acceptance, and peer Finished verification all exist. PeerHandshakeTranscriptCompleted remains a transcript milestone, not a trust or authentication claim.
procedure: Run the CRT requirement-home tests that cover the default-deny path, pinned-fingerprint positive and negative paths, explicit acceptance-state behavior, runtime propagation, and the tightened commit gate.

Run the RFC9001 requirement-home tests that still cover inbound Handshake packet processing, outbound Handshake packet emission, malformed-frame rejection, tampered-packet rejection, and wrong-material rejection for the bridge seam.

Inspect the bridge state and runtime integration to confirm that PeerCertificatePolicyAccepted remains explicit, no-policy configurations stay blocked, and PeerTransportParametersCommitted cannot occur before proof, policy acceptance, and peer Finished verification all exist.
scope: This slice verifies the permanent client-side certificate-acceptance policy seam and the tightened peer transport-parameter commit gate. It does not prove certificate trust, hostname validation, certificate-path validation, revocation, 0-RTT, 1-RTT, key update, or server-role handshake crypto.
preconditions: The tests project can access internal library seams through the existing friend assembly relationship.
